architecture.summary: Cascaded Sparse Autoencoders (CSAEs) decompose multimodal LLM visual representations into hierarchical, interpretable features. A first-level SAE is trained on model activations to extract low-level sparse features; a second-level SAE then trains directly on the decoder weights of the first SAE, treating learned feature directions as inputs to learn higher-level concept abstractions. Information flows from frozen MLLM activations → first-level SAE sparse codes → first-level decoder weights → second-level SAE sparse codes, enabling multi-level concept organization without nesting coupling or stacking bottlenecks.

biblio.title: Cascaded Sparse Autoencoders Learn Multi-Level Visual Concepts in Multimodal LLMs

claims.statement: CSAEs improve interpretability in terms of hierarchical concept coherence over state-of-the-art SAE baselines
The learned concept groups support effective group-level interventions in MLLM outputs
CSAEs avoid drawbacks from the shared-prefix coupling of nesting, Matryoshka-style hierarchies and the bottlenecks of naively stacked SAEs
CSAEs enable learning of 'concepts of concepts' by training a second-level SAE directly on the decoder weights of the first-level SAE
